heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Low recruitment rate | Medium | High | Multi-site recruitment, extended enrollment period (TBD months) |
| High dropout rate (>20%) | Medium | High | Retention strategies: reminders, incentives (TBD), flexible scheduling |
| LLM API downtime or cost overrun | Medium | Medium | Fallback to rule-based chatbot; budget contingency (TBD%) |
| Contamination between arms | Low | Medium | Cluster randomisation by site; clear separation of intervention/control workflows |
| Adverse events from LLM advice | Low | High | DSMB monitoring; stopping rule (AE rate >5%); medical disclaimer in system |
| Data privacy breach | Low | Very High | Encryption at rest/transit; access control; GDPR-compliant data handling; audit log |
| σ assumption incorrect (σ > 12) | Medium | High | Sensitivity analysis (σ=8,10,12); adaptive sample size re-estimation at interim (N=100) |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- Single-site design limits generalisability to other healthcare systems or populations
- Self-reported outcomes (PAM-13, MMAS-8, EQ-5D-5L) subject to recall and social desirability bias
- Co-design process may introduce selection bias (participants more engaged than general population)
- LLM-based intervention depends on third-party API (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic); changes in API availability or pricing may affect sustainability
- No blinding of participants or care providers due to nature of intervention; performance bias possible
- Follow-up limited to 6 months; long-term effects on activation and clinical outcomes unknown
- Contamination risk if control participants access similar LLM tools outside the study
- Assumed standard deviation (σ=10) based on limited prior data; true variability may differ, affecting power

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
- **Lead PI:** TBD (to be confirmed)
- **Co-Investigators:** TBD (to be confirmed)
- **Potential partners:**
  - Insignia Health (PAM-13 licence, patient activation expertise)
  - Fraunhofer IGD (LLM integration, explainable AI)
  - TSU (TBD University) — clinical site, recruitment
  - University of Copenhagen — health economics, EQ-5D-5L analysis
- **Roles:** Lead PI oversees trial design and execution; Co-Is contribute to specific work packages (e.g., LLM development, statistical analysis, patient engagement); partners provide licences, infrastructure, or recruitment sites.
- **Status:** Partnership agreements in negotiation; formal letters of support to be obtained before funding submission.
